{"credits": [{"type": "finder", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13433538?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/kerberosmansour", "id": 13433538, "login": "kerberosmansour", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjEzNDMzNTM4", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/kerberosmansour", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-48036", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 8.4, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:P/VC:N/VI:H/VA:L/SC:N/SI:H/SA:L"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-755", "name": "Improper Handling of Exceptional Conditions"}], "description": "**Affected:** `@hulumi/drift` `< 1.4.0` — **Fixed in:** `1.4.0` — **Severity:** Medium — **CWE-755 (Improper Handling of Exceptional Conditions)**

#### Summary

`@hulumi/drift` runs four adapters that each ask a different question about whether a resource has drifted (Pulumi-state diff, provider-version change, CloudTrail event, etc.). A classifier combines the adapters' answers into a verdict like `None / none`, `ConsoleBreakGlass / high`, or `Mixed / high`, and caches the verdict for 6 hours by default.

Two related bugs from one root cause — the classifier only read each adapter's `detected: true/false` field and ignored whether the adapter itself succeeded:

1. **Cached "all clear" on adapter failure.** When an adapter failed (e.g. transient network error from the Automation API), the classifier read `detected: false`, concluded "no drift", and cached the verdict as `None / none` for 6 hours. A single transient failure could mask real console-break-glass mutations for the rest of the window.
2. **Mixed verdicts without real evidence.** The `Mixed / high` and `ConsoleBreakGlass / high` verdicts (incident severity) could fire on the "the CloudTrail probe round-tripped successfully" signal rather than actual evidence that anything had been changed via the console. Normal provider-API churn could end up falsely escalated to incident severity.

#### Impact

Consumers running drift detection in CI / cron could see transient adapter failures silently cached as "all clear" — masking real attacks for up to six hours — or see ordinary provider-version churn falsely promoted to incident severity. Either way, the verdict source was unreliable for downstream incident workflows that gate on it.

#### Patches

Upgrade to `@hulumi/drift@1.4.0`. Classifier-only fix (the TLA+-verified 6-row verdict matrix is byte-identical):

- adapter failures now fail closed to `Unknown / low`, and degraded verdicts are not written to the cache;
- the `Mixed` / `ConsoleBreakGlass` promotion now requires real CloudTrail event evidence rather than probe liveness.

#### Workarounds

Setting `options.minConfidence: "medium"` on the classifier call prevents the degraded `None / none` from being cached (it doesn't meet the threshold), partially mitigating case (1). No workaround for case (2).

#### Resources

- [PR #178](https://github.com/kerberosmansour/hulumi/pull/178) (Cluster D); regression tests in `packages/drift/tests/classifier-fail-closed.test.ts`.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.0004, "percentile": 0.12464}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-32g3-35g9-wc9g", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-10T13:38:50Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-32g3-35g9-wc9g", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-32g3-35g9-wc9g"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-48036"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-06-10T13:38:50Z", "references": ["https://github.com/kerberosmansour/hulumi/security/advisories/GHSA-32g3-35g9-wc9g", "https://github.com/kerberosmansour/hulumi/pull/178", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-32g3-35g9-wc9g"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/kerberosmansour/hulumi/security-advisories/GHSA-32g3-35g9-wc9g", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/kerberosmansour/hulumi", "summary": "@hulumi/drift: Drift classifier fails open on adapter errors and over-promotes Mixed verdicts", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-10T13:38:50Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-32g3-35g9-wc9g", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.4.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "@hulumi/drift"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 1.4.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: @hulumi/drift: Drift classifier fails open on adapter errors and over-promotes Mixed verdicts
